pi_items.source: Code inspection of setup_task.sh: 10 doc.add_paragraph() calls create citations for Kahneman, Bandura, Milgram, Skinner, Piaget, Maslow, Vygotsky, Erikson, Bowlby, Pavlov
Verified against web search results for 'Albert Bandura Social Learning Theory 1977' and alphabetical sorting of 10 authors
Verified against web search results for 'John Bowlby Attachment and Loss 1969' and alphabetical sorting
Verified against web search results for 'Erik Erikson Childhood and Society 1950' and alphabetical sorting
Verified against web search results for 'Kahneman Tversky Prospect Theory 1979 Econometrica' and alphabetical sorting
Verified against web search results for 'Abraham Maslow A Theory of Human Motivation 1943' and alphabetical sorting
Verified against web search results for 'Stanley Milgram Behavioral Study of Obedience 1963' and alphabetical sorting
Verified against web search results for 'Ivan Pavlov Conditioned Reflexes 1927' and alphabetical sorting
Verified against web search results for 'Jean Piaget The Construction of Reality in the Child 1954' and alphabetical sorting
Verified against web search results for 'B.F. Skinner The Behavior of Organisms 1938' and alphabetical sorting
Verified against web search results for 'Lev Vygotsky Mind in Society 1978' and alphabetical sorting

privileged_info_summary: The task contains 10 real academic citations from well-known psychology publications, formatted intentionally in mixed incorrect styles (MLA, Chicago, informal). The expected first authors when sorted alphabetically are: Bandura, Bowlby, Erikson, Kahneman, Maslow, Milgram, Pavlov, Piaget, Skinner, and Vygotsky. All publications are verified real works: Kahneman & Tversky's 'Prospect Theory' (1979, Econometrica), Bandura's 'Social Learning Theory' (1977), Milgram's 'Behavioral Study of Obedience' (1963), Skinner's 'The Behavior of Organisms' (1938), Piaget's 'The Construction of Reality in the Child' (1954), Maslow's 'A Theory of Human Motivation' (1943), Vygotsky's 'Mind in Society' (1978), Erikson's 'Childhood and Society' (1950), Bowlby's 'Attachment and Loss Vol. 1' (1969), and Pavlov's 'Conditioned Reflexes' (1927).
